moments.text: Take a selfie at the bow right now — that's the Atlantic behind you.
Ask Holly what her favorite moment has been so far.
Order her favorite drink and leave it as a surprise at the suite.
Find somewhere quiet and just sit together for 10 minutes.
Tell her one thing you love about her today. Out loud.
Watch the sunset from the Haven Sundeck together tonight.
Get in the Haven pool together — no excuses, no phones.
Ask a crew member to take a proper photo of you two.
Write down three things you love about this trip so far. Share them with each other.
Order room service breakfast and eat it on the balcony.
Explore a part of the ship you haven't been to yet — together.
Hold hands while watching the ship pull into port.
Get off the ship first and find a quiet spot in port before the crowds.
Buy her something small and local — doesn't need to be expensive.
Take a photo of her when she's not looking. Candid shots are always the best ones.
Put your phone away for one full hour. Just be here.
Tell her about a memory from before the cruise that you want to hold onto.
Get a couples massage at Mandara Spa — check availability with the concierge.
Recreate the Titanic pose at the bow. You know you want to.
Sit at the Observation Lounge at night — it's usually quiet and the stars are incredible.
Share a dessert at one of the specialty restaurants tonight.
Ask her what she wants to do tomorrow — no agenda, just her call.
Walk the Waterfront promenade (Deck 8) — it circles the ship and has great views.
Stay up late tonight. One of the sea days will be the best night of the trip.
Text someone back home one photo that captures the trip so far.
Ask Holly what she wants to eat tonight — then make it happen.
Find the best view on the ship right now and stand there for 5 minutes.
Get ice cream together. It doesn't have to be complicated.
Dance to one song tonight — in the room counts.
Say thank you to each other. Specifically. Not just 'this is nice.'
